person.name: Olivier Calzi
person.bio: Olivier Calzi is a Site Reliability Engineer (Linux) and freelance IT Manager / Cloud Architect, with deep specialization in CNCF technologies and modern cloud-native infrastructure. He brings hands-on expertise in production-grade Kubernetes environments, with a strong focus on platform resilience, security, and DevOps culture. With experience across both startup and enterprise ecosystems, Olivier contributes actively to the Cloud Native community through mentoring, technical writing, and open collaboration. He is passionate about building robust, scalable systems and continuously advancing the state of cloud operations.

person.name: Pramodh Kumar M
person.bio: As a seasoned DevSecOps Consultant with over five years of hands-on experience, I specialize in implementing and optimizing solutions that encompass a range of cloud services and platforms, including GitHub Enterprise, Azure DevOps, LaunchDarkly, Octopus Deploy, SonarQube, JFrog Platform, Atlassian Stack, as well as HashiCorp Terraform and Vault. My expertise is focused on delivering customized solutions that maximize value for clients, drawing on a comprehensive knowledge of DevSecOps best practices.

person.name: Roy Liang
person.bio: I am a cloud native developer and advocator with over 6 years of experience in cloud native community. I was a cloud native infrastructure SRE and early Kubernetes contributor. I am currently working as a Senior R&D Engineer in Machine Learning Platform team at Tencent. I have participated in multiple cloud native projects and now I am working on cloud native AI infrastructure. I am also a co-author of "Istio Handbook", traslator of "Distributed Machine Learning Pattern" and speaker of KubeCon.

person.name: Vino Alex
person.bio: I am a technologist with over 20 years of exceptional contributions to the IT industry, specializing in Cloud-Native solutions, Application modernization, Machine Learning, and Digital transformation initiatives.I possess deep expertise in Cloud Native technologies, focusing on OpenShift, Kubernetes, and service mesh, and a comprehensive understanding of leading public cloud platforms.  You may find some of my passionate ramblings at https://vinothecloudone.medium.com/

person.name: Wojciech Baranowski
person.bio: Cloud Solution Architect at Gdańsk University of Technology, working at the intersection of R&D and production-grade infrastructure. I design and build cloud-native systems from the ground up, focusing on Kubernetes-based platforms and complex distributed architectures. My work centers on solving non-trivial engineering problems and turning advanced concepts into reliable, scalable environments.

person.name: Heechul Kim
person.bio: As a senior full-stack developer with over 20 years of experience at Samsung SDS,  I am passionate about Kubernetes and the cloud native ecosystem.  In particular, I am very interested in building and optimizing DevOps environments for generative AI and RAG implementations.

person.name: Kai Huei Lim
person.bio: Kai Huei is a Senior DevOps Engineer based in Singapore, specializing in Kubernetes, CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), GitOps, and observability. He is passionate about AI, cloud-native innovation, and automation, and is dedicated to bridging cutting-edge technologies with practical implementations to build reliable, scalable, and robust platforms.  Let’s connect, collaborate, and build the future of cloud-native — together. 🌍

person.name: Mauricio Salatino
person.bio: I am an Open Source Software Engineer at @Diagrid, contributing to and driving initiatives for the Dapr OSS project. I also serve as a Steering Committee member for the Knative Project, and I am Co-Leading the Knative Functions initiative. I've been writing on my blog, salaboy.com for over 15 years and I just published a book titled Platform Engineering on Kubernetes for Manning. I previously worked at VMware and Red Hat, building tools to help developers be more productive.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I've been working on Cloud Native and CNCF communities for over seven years now, and I've been constantly trying to give back and connect people who share the same interests around open source projects. Being an ambassador allows me to expand my network to keep connecting, mentoring, and guiding community members to grow.

person.name: Pontus Ullgren
person.bio: Longtime Linux user 🐧, software developer, software architect and Platform Engineer. Constantly exploring new technologies and methodologies. I'm particularly interested by the challenges and opportunities within Identity and Access Management (IAM) and API management.  Favorite quote: sed/awk: changing the world one line at a time

person.name: Andrei Ivantsov
person.bio: I am a passionate software engineer with deep expertise in cloud-native technologies, specializing in Kubernetes to build scalable, resilient, and secure architectures. With a strong focus on continuous improvement, I leverage state-of-the-art technologies to drive innovation throughout the software lifecycle. As a team player, I thrive in collaborative environments, fostering knowledge sharing and best practices. Committed to software craftsmanship, I view the IT industry as a lifelong learning journey, always embracing new challenges and advancements.

person.name: Cedrick Vaccard
person.bio: ⸻  I am a Microsoft-certified Cloud and Kubernetes engineer with extensive experience in cloud-native and containerized environments, including hands-on work with Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). Through my background in Microsoft ecosystems, I have developed strong expertise in system automation, infrastructure operations, and platform reliability. I focus on designing and operating production-grade environments with particular attention to identity integration, networking, and workload security, always driven by a deep curiosity to understand how systems truly work under the hood.Outside of technology, I am a marathon runner, and the discipline, endurance, and long-term commitment required in endurance sports naturally shape the way I approach complex technical challenges.

person.name: Kashif Karim
person.bio: As a DevOps engineer with a passion for cloud-native technologies, I've spent the past few years honing my skills in deploying and managing applications. My recent collaboration with a government organization involved transitioning their applications from Docker Compose to Kubernetes, optimizing their infrastructure for scalability and reliability. Alongside my professional endeavors, I'm committed to mentoring and guiding individuals on the CNCF certification path, helping them navigate the complexities of cloud-native environments with ease. While I don't run Meetups or maintain a personal blog, I'm always eager to share my knowledge and experience with others in the community.

person.name: Michael Stucki
person.bio: I am a Linux engineer with a primary focus on Kubernetes, OpenShift, and cloud-native technologies. My work revolves around designing, implementing, and maintaining scalable, reliable cloud-native infrastructures, combining my Linux expertise with advanced container orchestration solutions. In addition to my professional focus, I contribute to IT education by evaluating practical work from IT learners in the Canton of Bern, ensuring training quality standards. I am also involved in the SwissSkills Cloud Computing competitions, where I help design tasks, evaluate participants, and drive the event's success. These roles allow me to balance my technical career with fostering the next generation of IT professionals.
person.company: Federal Department of Defence, Civil Protection and Sport (DDPS)

person.name: Ronit Banerjee
person.bio: I am Ronit Banerjee, a pre-final year Computer Science Engineering Undergraduate student. Along with my academics, I have maintained a strong portfolio being an Open Source Developer at the projects incubated and graduated from The Linux Foundation and Cloud Native Computing Foundation. My enthusiasm with DevOps and Cloud Native helped me to secure scholarships like Travel Funding Scholarship and Dan Kohn Scholarship by The Linux Foundation and Cloud Native Computing Foundation to attend flagship conferences such as KubeCon + CloudNativeCon + Open Source Summit China 2023 at Shanghai and Open Source Summit Europe 2022 at Dublin. I also worked as an Open Source Developer in Google Summer of Code (GSoC) at DBpedia Association. Apart from my technical experience, I got my hands dirty in fostering the Cloud Native Ecosystem. I am also the Chapter Lead at Cloud Native Hooghly which is an official CNCF Chapter listed amongst 40 such clubs in the Asia Pacific Region. Right now, with the proliferation of the Cloud Native Tools, I successfully turned the chapter to be the fastest growing in the entire Eastern India, catering 250+ Active members. I am also a Sig Contribex's Comms Volunteer for Kubernetes Contributors Summit North America 2023 at Chicago, United States of America primarily looking into the “Call for Proposal” emails and notifying about the updates to the KubeCon co-located event attendees. I have significant experience in pitching tech products to Venture Capitalists and have successfully secured grants from Government of India twice for my deep-tech startup.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I always wanted to boost Cloud Native Ecosystem in my locality whereas I felt there is a gap of awareness. Being an ambassador, it would really help me to reach areas where I couldn't do by myself just being a CNCG chapter lead of Hooghly. With CNCF's support it would be possible.

person.name: Gelu
person.bio: DevOps Enginnering, OpenSource enthusiast and keen to learn everyday new stuff

person.name: Jintao Zhang
person.bio: Jintao is a Sr. SE at Kong Inc. Kubernetes Ingress-NGINX maintainer. Microsoft MVP. Public speaker and author. He was speaking at multiple conferences, including KCD, IstioCon, DockerCon, and more. He enjoys sharing technology and has been actively involved in open-source projects for a long time.

person.name: May Thiri Aung
person.bio: I'm a passionate DevOps and Cloud Native engineer based in Japan, specializing in Kubernetes, container security, GitOps, and cloud infrastructure.
